title: nephrodium filix-mas var. cochleatum
intro: Nephrodium filix-mas var. cochleatum, also known as the kidney fern or adder's tongue fern, is a variety of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as rheumatism, arthritis, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Nephrodium filix-mas var. cochleatum are said to include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and providing antioxidant properties, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it can also be dried and used in teas or smoked for its medicinal benefits.
